channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Residents slog through flooded streets, clear debris after Hurricane Milton tore through Florida
item.description: LITHIA, Fla. (AP) — Florida residents slogged through flooded streets, gathered up scattered debris and assessed damage to their homes on Friday after Hurricane Milton smashed through coastal communities and spawned a barrage of deadly tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS O'MEARA, BRENDAN FARRINGTON and TY O'NEIL, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Montana businessman gets 2 years in prison for role in Jan. 6, 2021, insurrection at the US Capitol
item.description: HELENA, Mont. (AP) — A Montana business owner and supporter of former President Donald Trump has been sentenced to two years in federal prison for his role in the Jan. 6, 2021, insurrection at the U.S. Capitol that interrupted certification of the 2020 Electoral College vote.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 7-Eleven to close over 400 locations in North America
item.description: Hundreds of “underperforming” 7-Eleven stores across North America are closing, the convenience store announced Thursday. Seven & I Holdings, 7-Eleven’s Japan-based parent company, revealed in its earnings report that 444 7-Eleven locations are closing.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: What's behind the northern lights that dazzled the sky farther south than normal
item.description: Another in a series of unusually strong solar storms hitting Earth produced stunning skies full of pinks, purples, greens and blues farther south than normal, including into parts of Germany, the United Kingdom, New England and New York City.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Krispy Kreme giving away free doughnuts to celebrate its expanded partnership with McDonald's
item.description: Thanks to the expanded partnership between Krispy Kreme and McDonald’s, consumers can get a sweet deal heading into the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Michigan newspaper issues correction after Trump claims he won Man of Year award
item.description: A Michigan newspaper issued a correction after former President Donald Trump cited the paper at a speech in Detroit claiming he was Michigan’s “Man of the Year” in 2023.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Family sues after finding out on TikTok funeral home sent mom's body to wrong country
item.description: A family in New York City is suing a funeral home for mistakenly sending their mother Carmen Maldonado's casket to Guatemala for burial instead of Ecuador.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Residents slog through flooded streets, pick up debris after Milton
item.description: Florida residents slogged through flooded streets, gathered up scattered debris and assessed damage to their homes on Friday after Hurricane Milton smashed through coastal communities and tore homes to pieces, flooded streets and spawned a barrage of deadly tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ethel Kennedy, social activist and wife of Robert F. Kennedy, has died
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Ethel Kennedy, the wife of Sen. Robert F. Kennedy, who raised their 11 children after he was assassinated and remained dedicated to social causes and the family’s legacy for decades thereafter, died on Thursday, her family said. She was 96.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and STEVE LeBLANC, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Internet Archive data breach exposes more than 31 million user accounts: reports
item.description: The Internet Archive, a popular digital library known for its Wayback Machine, was hacked and suffered a breach that reportedly exposed the data of 31 million user accounts.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, Fox 5

item.title: Hurricane Milton strikes Florida, officials warn of hurricane relief scams
item.description: As relief efforts continue following Hurricanes Helene and Milton, the U.S. Department of Justice is alerting the public to a rise in scams aimed at exploiting victims of these disasters.
item.dc:creator: Sahara Sriraman, NewsNation

item.title: Hurricane Milton disrupts Yom Kippur plans for Jews in Florida
item.description: WINTER PARK, Florida (AP) — Many Jews worldwide will mark Yom Kippur in fasting and prayer at their synagogues this weekend.
item.dc:creator: MARIAM FAM, GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO and HOLLY MEYER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Viral Tampa sensation 'Lieutenant Dan,' who rode out Hurricane Milton in boat, describes how he survived
item.description: A Florida man named Joseph Malinowski, referred to by Tampa locals as "Lt. Dan," went viral on TikTok before his criminal background was revealed to users.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Coast Guard video shows helicopter crew rescue man floating on a cooler 30 miles off Florida coast after storm
item.description: A man clinging to a cooler 30 miles off the coast of Longboat Key, Florida, was rescued by a Coast Guard helicopter crew the day after Hurricane Milton battered the state.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Photos: First look at Siesta Key after Hurricane Milton
item.description: Aerial images show the damage in Siesta Key, and in Osprey, which is located just inland of the island.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Klynstra, NewsNation

item.title: Trump tells Detroit he'll make car loan interest tax-deductible
item.description: Former President Trump laid out his pitch to strengthen the auto industry in an economic address in Detroit on Thursday, proposing to make interest on car loans fully tax deductible, but did not explain how the proposed changes would be paid for.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Flesh eating bacteria thrives after hurricanes. Here's what to know
item.description: "You step on a nail, slice your leg, you get an open wound and the bacteria gets in,” said an infectious disease expert.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, KRON4

item.title: CNN's Anderson Cooper hit by flying object during Milton live shot
item.description: CNN’s Anderson Cooper was hit by a flying object on Wednesday during a live shot while reporting from Florida on Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, ABC 4

item.title: Social Security announces 2.5 percent cost-of-living adjustment for 2025
item.description: Social Security recipients will see a 2.5 percent bump as part of their cost-of-living adjustment (COLA) for 2025.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, KRON4

item.title: Video: Hurricane Milton sucks water out of Tampa Bay
item.description: Tampa Bay experienced a rare reverse storm surge as Hurricane Milton made landfall Wednesday evening.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, KRON4

item.title: Helene spurs IV fluid shortage. Milton could make it worse
item.description: Hospitals across the U.S. are feeling the impact of Hurricane Helene as experts warn of an imminent intravenous (IV) solution shortage due to the temporary shutdown of a facility in North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: Florida has nearly all ballots counted on Election Day, while California can take weeks. This is why
item.description: Why is it that some states have nearly all the ballots counted within hours of polls closing while others take weeks?
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Beloved father fighting for life in ICU after house explosion in Long Beach
item.description: The man injured in a violent house explosion on Monday is fighting for his life in the intensive care unit, his family tells KTLA.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, Fox 5

item.title: A retired Houston officer gets 60 years in a couple's drug raid deaths that revealed corruption
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A former Houston police officer was sentenced to 60 years in prison on Tuesday for the murder of a married couple during a drug raid that revealed systemic corruption in the department’s narcotics unit and prompted criticism of the law enforcement agency and its tactics.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: TikTok is designed to be addictive to kids and causes them harm, US states' lawsuits say
item.description: More than a dozen states and the District of Columbia filed lawsuits against TikTok on Tuesday, saying that the popular short-form video app is designed to be addictive to kids and harms their mental health.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: How will Hurricane Milton stack up against other major recent storms?
item.description: As a ferocious Hurricane Milton charges its way across the Gulf Coast with Tampa and St. Petersburg in its sights, thousands flee the area for safer ground, leaving behind debris from Hurricane Helene's destruction less that two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why do people fill their bathtubs with water before a hurricane?
item.description: If you live in a hurricane-prone area, it may be common practice for you. If you live well outside that region, you may be confused by the move.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Biden sets 10-year deadline for US cities to replace lead pipes nationwide
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A decade after the Flint, Michigan, water crisis raised alarms about the continuing dangers of lead in tap water, President Joe Biden on Tuesday set a 10-year deadline for cities across the nation to replace their lead pipes, finalizing an aggressive approach aimed at ensuring that
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY and MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pioneers in artificial intelligence win the Nobel Prize in physics
item.description: STOCKHOLM (AP) — Two pioneers of artificial intelligence — John Hopfield and Geoffrey Hinton — won the Nobel Prize in physics Tuesday for helping create the building blocks of machine learning that is revolutionizing the way we work and live but also creates new threats for humanity.
item.dc:creator: DANIEL NIEMANN, SETH BORENSTEIN and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What makes a storm a hurricane? The dangers across 5 categories
item.description: Severe weather can target any part of the globe, but each year the Atlantic hurricane season brings heavy rain, strong wind, storm surge and sometimes catastrophic devastation to locations from the Caribbean islands and Central America all the way up through the United States and into Canada.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: West Virginia woman met her husband after getting donor lungs from his late sister
item.description: A woman from West Virginia got a new chance at life and found the love of her life thanks to organ donation.
item.dc:creator: Sam Kirk, WGN 9

item.title: Airline apologizes after playing explicit movie on every screen
item.description: Australia's Qantas Airways is apologizing after showing a movie that contains scenes of nudity, graphic language and sexting to the passengers aboard a recent flight from Sydney to Haneda, Japan.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: This Montana Senate candidate said his opponent ate 'lobbyist steak.' But he lobbied—with steak
item.description: BILLINGS, Mont. (AP) — During an October debate, Montana GOP Senate candidate Tim Sheehy denounced his Democratic opponent, Sen. Jon Tester, for ” eating lobbyist steak ″ while the Republican fought in Afghanistan.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Are hurricanes intensifying more rapidly than ever before?
item.description: Rapid intensification is defined by the National Hurricane Center as an increase of at least 30 knots (about 35 mph) in maximum sustained winds within a 24-hour period, which can mean a jump of two categories on the Saffir-Simpson scale.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WGN 9

item.title: Nobel Prize in medicine honors 2 scientists for their discovery of microRNA
item.description: STOCKHOLM (AP) — Two scientists won the Nobel Prize in physiology or medicine on Monday for their discovery of microRNA, tiny bits of genetic material that serve as on and off switches inside cells that help control what the cells do and when they do it.
item.dc:creator: DANIEL NIEMANN, MARIA CHENG and MIKE CORDER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Where do Trump, Harris stand on marijuana policy?
item.description: In a poll taken last year, roughly 70% of American adults said marijuana should be legal — but where do the presidential candidates stand?
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What happens to items surrendered to TSA?
item.description: The Transportation Security Administration (TSA) said it does not confiscate items. According to the agency, if someone shows up with a prohibited item, they are given a choice on what to do with it.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, WFLA, NBC
